There is no AREAS whitelist enforcement (arbitrary `area` text accepted), no upper bound on `hours` (e.g. 99999 -> $2M booking) or fractional-hour floor, and no rejection of past `scheduled_at`. `new Date(scheduledLocal)` can also produce an Invalid Date that throws on .toISOString(). This allows malformed/abusive bookings and business-rule bypass.
findings.fix: Validate server-side before the RPC: assert `area` ∈ AREAS (src/lib/areas.ts), clamp hours to a sane range (e.g. 1–24, integer/half-hour), require scheduled_at to be a valid future timestamp, and reject empty full_address. Mirror the same checks inside request_booking so direct RPC calls are also guarded.

findings.title: No rate limiting on booking broadcast, messaging, checkout, or auth
findings.problem: There is no rate limiting anywhere in the app or DB. request_booking fans out an offer row (and a realtime ring) to every active verified cleaner, so a single user can spam-create bookings to flood/abuse all cleaners; send_booking_message, payDeposit/payBalance (Stripe session creation), and review/dispute creation are likewise unthrottled, and login has no app-level throttle. This enables abuse/DoS and unnecessary Stripe/email cost.
findings.fix: Introduce per-user/IP rate limiting (e.g. Upstash Ratelimit or a Postgres counter) on submitBooking, sendMessage, startCheckout, openDispute and the mark-read route; cap concurrent 'broadcasting' bookings per customer.

findings.title: cancel_booking trusts client-supplied actor label (p_by spoofing)
findings.problem: cancel_booking authorizes via `customer_id = auth.uid() OR is_admin()` (good) but writes `cancelled_by = p_by` from the caller. A customer can call the RPC directly with `p_by:'admin'` or `'cleaner'` or `'system'` (the table check only restricts the enum set, not the truthfulness), corrupting the cancellation audit trail and any logic/UI that trusts who cancelled.
findings.fix: Derive the actor inside the function instead of trusting the parameter: set `cancelled_by = case when is_admin() then 'admin' else 'customer' end` and drop p_by from the signature (or ignore it).

findings.title: Dispute/refund webhook and admin refund write to non-existent columns — financial audit trail silently lost
findings.problem: The charge.dispute.created handler inserts into `disputes` with columns that do not exist in the schema (payment_id, stripe_dispute_id, reason, amount) and omits the table's NOT NULL columns (raised_by, category, description) per 0008_improvements.sql:54-64; admin issueRefund (src/app/admin/disputes/[id]/actions.ts:75-85) inserts payments with non-existent `payment_type`/`notes`/`updated_at` and updateDisputeStatus sets a non-existent `updated_at`. All of these throw at runtime and are swallowed (webhook returns 200, action errors), so Stripe-side chargebacks and issued refunds are never recorded in the DB — a security-relevant loss of financial/dispute audit data and a reconciliation gap. (Related schema drift: bookings/[id]/page.tsx:104 filters reviews on `reviewer_id`, and admin/cleaners pages query a non-existent `offers` table and `reviews.cleaner_id`, so 'already reviewed' detection and cleaner stats are broken.)
findings.fix: Add a migration aligning the disputes/payments tables with the code (e.g. add stripe_dispute_id, payment_id, amount, reason to disputes; add a 'refund' payment_type and notes/updated_at to payments) OR fix the inserts to match the current schema and supply all NOT NULL columns; fix the reviewer_id/offers/cleaner_id queries to match real columns.

findings.title: Reviews are world-readable including free-text comment and booking_id
findings.problem: `reviews_select for select using (true)` exposes every review row to anon/any user, including the free-text `comment` (which has no length cap and may contain PII the customer typed) and the `booking_id`. submitReview (src/app/bookings/[id]/review/actions.ts) does not bound comment length. While bookings RLS still protects the booking itself, this leaks all review prose and enumerable booking IDs to the public.
findings.fix: Either surface ratings only through the existing SECURITY DEFINER get_cleaner_card aggregate and tighten reviews_select (e.g. drop booking_id from public exposure via a view), or at minimum cap comment length (e.g. <=1000) and consider not exposing raw booking_id publicly.

findings.title: mark-read route is a cookie-authenticated state-changing POST with no origin/CSRF check
findings.problem: Unlike Next.js Server Actions (which have built-in same-origin enforcement), this route handler performs a cookie-authenticated state change (marking a notification read) with no Origin/Referer or CSRF-token check. Impact is low (it only flips read_at for the caller's own rows), but it is the one mutation that bypasses the Server Action CSRF protection used everywhere else.
findings.fix: Validate the Origin header against the app origin (or require a CSRF token), or convert mark-read to a Server Action like markAllRead so it inherits framework CSRF protection.

result.summary: TypeScript type-checks clean (tsc --noEmit exits 0), but there is a cluster of high-impact runtime bugs caused by code querying/writing columns that do not exist in the actual Postgres schema. The most serious is the admin refund flow: it issues the Stripe refund successfully, then the DB record insert ALWAYS fails (wrong column `payment_type`, invalid enum value 'refund', and nonexistent `notes`/`updated_at` columns) and the error is swallowed — money leaves Stripe with no recorded trace. The admin dispute-resolution update also targets a nonexistent `updated_at` column and fails entirely, so admins cannot resolve disputes; and the dispute page's payments query uses `payment_type`, so the refund UI always shows "no payments to refund". The Stripe webhook's `charge.dispute.created` handler inserts disputes with a completely wrong column set (and omits NOT NULL `raised_by`/`category`/`description`), so it always fails silently. The customer booking page checks `reviews.reviewer_id` (nonexistent), so duplicate-review detection never works and re-submission hits a unique-constraint error. There is also a genuine timezone bug (datetime-local parsed in server TZ, not the customer's Pacific TZ), duplicate notifications on Stripe webhook replay, swallowed RPC errors in start/complete job, an over-restrictive profiles RLS policy that prevents booking counterparties from ever seeing each other's names in jobs/chat, and a known duplicate MessagePanel component. Realtime subscriptions are all correctly cleaned up (no leaks).

findings.title: Admin refund records to nonexistent columns/enum — Stripe refund succeeds but DB write silently fails
findings.problem: issueRefund() calls stripe.refunds.create() FIRST, then inserts into `payments` with `payment_type: 'refund'`, `notes`, `updated_at`, `created_at`. The payments table (migration 0005) has column `type` (not `payment_type`), the `type` enum is only ('deposit','balance') so 'refund' is invalid, and `notes`/`updated_at` columns do not exist. The required NOT NULL `type` is never supplied. The insert ALWAYS errors, but the result is never checked, so the error is swallowed. Net effect: money is refunded in Stripe with zero DB record, no booking/dispute status change, and no error surfaced to the admin.
findings.fix: Change the insert to use the real columns: `type` (you must first add 'refund' to the payment_type enum, or model refunds with a negative-amount 'balance'/'deposit' row + a separate refunds table), drop `notes`/`updated_at` (no such columns; add a `notes` column via migration if needed), and check `error` and surface failures. Also wrap so the Stripe refund and DB write are reconciled (e.g. record an intent row before calling Stripe).

findings.title: Admin dispute status update targets nonexistent `updated_at` — update always fails, disputes can't be resolved
findings.problem: updateDisputeStatus() sets `updated_at: new Date().toISOString()` in the disputes UPDATE. The disputes table (migration 0008) has no `updated_at` column, so the entire update is rejected by Postgres. The result/error is not checked, so it fails silently — status, resolution and resolved_at are never persisted. Admins literally cannot mark a dispute investigating/resolved/closed.
findings.fix: Remove the `updated_at` key from the update object (or add an `updated_at timestamptz` column + trigger via migration if you want audit timestamps). Also check the returned `error` and surface it.

findings.title: Admin dispute page payments query uses wrong column `payment_type` — refund panel always empty
findings.problem: The payments query selects `payment_type` and filters `.neq('payment_type','refund')`; the column is actually `type`. The query errors, `payments` is null, and the page renders 'No paid payments available to refund.' So the admin can never even open the refund form. Line 211 also renders `{p.payment_type}` which would be undefined. (Note the admin BOOKINGS detail page at src/app/admin/bookings/[id]/page.tsx:82 correctly uses `type` with a comment, confirming this is a regression.)
findings.fix: Replace `payment_type` with `type` in the select, the .neq filter, and the option label. Since 'refund' isn't a valid value of `type` yet, reconsider the .neq filter accordingly.

findings.title: Stripe webhook charge.dispute.created inserts disputes with wrong schema — always fails, booking flagged with no dispute row
findings.problem: The handler inserts into `disputes` with columns payment_id, stripe_dispute_id, reason, amount, status, created_at. The disputes table (migration 0008) has none of payment_id/stripe_dispute_id/reason/amount, and requires NOT NULL raised_by, category, description which are not provided. The insert always errors; the error is unchecked. Meanwhile the booking IS updated to status 'disputed' (line 154-158), so a Stripe chargeback leaves a booking stuck in 'disputed' with no corresponding dispute record for admins to action. There is also no idempotency guard, so a replayed event would re-mark/re-insert.
findings.fix: Either add the missing columns to disputes via migration (payment_id, stripe_dispute_id, reason, amount, and make raised_by/category/description nullable or supply system defaults) or map the Stripe dispute onto the existing schema (category='payment_issue', description=dispute.reason, raised_by=customer_id from booking). Add a unique index on stripe_dispute_id and upsert for idempotency, and check the error.

findings.title: Booking time stored in wrong timezone (server TZ instead of customer's Pacific)
findings.problem: The form sends a `datetime-local` string (no timezone, e.g. '2026-06-26T14:00'). submitBooking does `new Date(scheduledLocal).toISOString()` on the SERVER, which interprets the naive string in the server's local timezone. On Vercel/most hosts the server runs in UTC, so a Courtenay BC customer (Pacific, UTC-7/-8) who picks 2:00pm gets stored as 14:00 UTC = 7:00am Pacific. Every booking time is off by 7-8 hours, shown to both customer and cleaner.
findings.fix: Capture the customer's timezone offset on the client (hidden input or convert in a client component using the browser's Date), or store the local wall-clock + an explicit IANA tz (e.g. 'America/Vancouver') and convert deterministically. Do not rely on server-local parsing of a naive datetime-local value.

findings.title: Duplicate-review check queries nonexistent `reviews.reviewer_id` — prompt never clears, resubmit errors
findings.problem: hasReview is computed via `.from('reviews').select('id',{count:'exact',head:true}).eq('booking_id',id).eq('reviewer_id',user.id)`. The reviews table (migration 0006) has columns id, booking_id, rating, comment, created_at — there is no `reviewer_id`. The query errors, count is null, hasReview is always false, so the 'How was your cleaning? Leave a review' prompt is shown even after the customer has reviewed. Clicking it again calls submitReview, which violates the `booking_id` UNIQUE constraint and throws an unhandled error to the user.
findings.fix: Drop the `.eq('reviewer_id', user.id)` filter (the booking_id is already unique per booking, and the page already verified customer ownership). Just check existence by booking_id.

findings.title: Duplicate notifications on Stripe webhook replay
findings.problem: The payment upsert is idempotent (onConflict stripe_session_id, ignoreDuplicates) and the booking status update is gated by prior status, but the createNotification calls fire unconditionally every time checkout.session.completed is delivered. Stripe legitimately re-delivers/retries events, so customers and cleaners get duplicate 'deposit paid'/'deposit received'/'balance received' notifications.
findings.fix: Only send notifications when the status update actually transitioned a row. Use `.update(...).eq('status','accepted').select('id')` and send notifications only if the returned array is non-empty (i.e. this delivery performed the transition).

findings.title: startJob/completeJob swallow RPC errors (fire-and-forget)
findings.problem: Unlike acceptJob/declineJob, startJob and completeJob call supabase.rpc without destructuring or checking `error`, then immediately revalidatePath. If the RPC fails (e.g. status guard not met, or the SECURITY DEFINER function raises), the failure is invisible: the UI just refreshes and the button reappears with no feedback. The underlying SQL functions also no-op silently when the WHERE guard (cleaner_id/status) doesn't match.
findings.fix: Destructure `{ error }` from both rpc calls and `throw new Error(error.message)` on failure, matching acceptJob/declineJob. Consider returning a boolean of whether the transition occurred.

findings.title: Profiles RLS blocks booking counterparties from reading each other's names
findings.problem: profiles_select policy is `id = auth.uid() OR is_admin()`. So when the cleaner jobs page embeds `profiles!bookings_customer_id_fkey(name)` (src/app/cleaner/jobs/page.tsx:68, jobs/[id]/page.tsx:64) the join returns null and customerName always falls back to 'Customer'. Likewise MessagePanel fetches the sender's profile name (src/app/bookings/[id]/MessagePanel.tsx:60-64, src/components/MessagePanel.tsx:62-66); the other party's row is unreadable, so chat shows 'Cleaner'/'Unknown' instead of real names. The customer booking page only sees the cleaner name because it goes through the SECURITY DEFINER get_cleaner_card RPC, masking how broken the direct path is.
findings.fix: Add an RLS policy allowing a user to read the name of a profile they share a booking with (customer<->assigned cleaner), or expose names via a SECURITY DEFINER RPC like get_cleaner_card for both directions. Then the embedded joins/sender lookups will resolve.

findings.title: Duplicate, divergent MessagePanel components
findings.problem: Two MessagePanel implementations exist and diverge: components/MessagePanel.tsx (used by the cleaner job detail page) calls the send_booking_message RPC directly, caps input at 500 chars, and shows relative time; app/bookings/[id]/MessagePanel.tsx (used by the customer page) calls the sendMessage server action, caps at 1000, and shows clock time. They also create the browser client differently (components version calls createClient() in the render body on every render). This is drift-prone duplicated logic.
findings.fix: Delete one and standardize on a single shared component (prefer the server-action variant for consistent revalidation, and create the Supabase client inside useEffect/useRef rather than per-render). Align the char limit with the DB CHECK (booking_messages body BETWEEN 1 AND 1000).

findings.title: JS-free refund form always uses the first payment's intent/amount, not the selected one
findings.problem: The payment <select> has no JS wiring (onChange is a no-op), but the hidden `stripe_payment_intent_id` field and the amount `max` are hardcoded to `payments?.[0]`. If a booking has multiple paid payments (deposit + balance), choosing the second option still refunds against the first payment's intent and validates against the wrong max. (Currently moot because the payments query is broken, but it will misfire once that's fixed.)
findings.fix: Make the panel a small client component that updates the hidden intent and amount max from the selected option, or submit the payment_id only and have issueRefund look up the intent/amount server-side from the chosen payment row.

findings.title: Leftover console.log statements in Stripe webhook
findings.problem: Several console.log calls for dispute/refund debugging remain in the production webhook handler (lines 171, 193, 199, 201). console.error at 208/mark-read 38 is acceptable; the console.log lines are noise and may leak booking/payment ids into logs.
findings.fix: Remove the console.log calls or route them through a leveled logger; keep console.error for genuine failures.

findings.title: Deposit rounding mismatch between computePrice/UI (whole units) and DB (2 decimals)
findings.problem: computePrice rounds the deposit with Math.round((total*depositPercent)/100) — to a whole dollar — and the booking form preview uses the same Math.round((rate*3*depositPercent)/100). The authoritative DB function request_booking (migration 0003:119) computes round(v_total * v_deposit_pct/100.0, 2) to 2 decimals. For non-integer results the customer-facing estimate can differ by cents from the amount actually charged. computePrice is currently only exercised by types/tests, but the divergent rounding is a latent money-display inconsistency.
findings.fix: Round to 2 decimals consistently (Math.round(x*100)/100) in computePrice and the book page preview so the displayed deposit matches the stored/charged deposit_amount.

findings.title: Marking notifications read doesn't refresh the nav unread badge
findings.problem: markAllRead revalidates only '/notifications'. The unread count bubble is rendered by the layout-level Nav (src/components/Nav.tsx) which counts unread notifications. After 'Mark all read', the bubble in the navbar still shows the old count until the next full navigation, because the layout segment isn't revalidated.
findings.fix: Call revalidatePath('/', 'layout') (or revalidate the relevant layout) in markAllRead and the mark-read route so the Nav unread count recomputes.

findings.title: Admin reassignCleaner only sets cleaner_id, leaving status/offers inconsistent
findings.problem: reassignCleaner updates only `cleaner_id`. It doesn't adjust status, create/accept a booking_offers row for the new cleaner, or notify anyone. A booking can end up with a cleaner_id while status is still 'broadcasting' (or with a new cleaner who has no offer row and no address access path consistent with the offer model), and the new cleaner gets no notification.
findings.fix: In reassignCleaner, also set an appropriate status, upsert an accepted booking_offers row for the new cleaner (and mark the old one), and send a notification. Ideally implement as a SECURITY DEFINER RPC mirroring accept_offer semantics.

findings.title: Add deposit deadline enforcement so accepted bookings can't hang forever
findings.problem: A `deposit_deadline` column exists on bookings but is never set or enforced anywhere. accept_offer (migration 0003:145) moves a booking to 'accepted' with no deadline, and nothing expires it. If the customer never pays the deposit, the booking sits in 'accepted' indefinitely, the assigned cleaner is blocked from other matching, and the address stays masked with no resolution.
findings.fix: Set deposit_deadline = now() + interval (e.g. 1h) when a cleaner accepts, and add a scheduled job/cron (or a guarded RPC invoked on read) that re-broadcasts or moves expired accepted bookings back to broadcasting / no_cleaner_found and notifies the customer.

findings.title: Notify the customer when a cleaner accepts the job
findings.problem: When a cleaner accepts (accept_offer), the customer only finds out via the StatusLive realtime page refresh while that page is open. No notification row is created, unlike deposit_paid/balance_paid which do notify. A customer who isn't currently viewing the booking page never learns a cleaner was found until they revisit.
findings.fix: In accept_offer (or the acceptJob server action) create a notification for booking.customer_id ('Cleaner found — confirm with your deposit', booking_id) so it appears in the bell/notifications list.

result.summary: The app has solid bones (semantic landmarks: per-page <main>, <nav>, <footer>; correct html lang="en"; most form inputs are wrapped in <label>; sitemap.ts and robots.ts exist; root + /about have metadata), but several material gaps remain. Error-resilience is the weakest area: there are NO error boundaries (app/error.tsx, app/global-error.tsx) and NO app/not-found.tsx, so any thrown Supabase/render error or a notFound() call drops the user onto an unstyled, brand-less default page with no recovery. There are also no loading.tsx boundaries despite every page being an async server component running multiple sequential Supabase queries. Accessibility has one outright defect — the global .btn-primary/.btn-secondary :focus rules call outline:none and then use non-existent CSS properties (ring/ring-color/ring-offset are Tailwind shorthands, not CSS), so keyboard users get NO visible focus ring on the primary action buttons (WCAG 2.4.7) — plus a couple of unlabeled textareas, a mobile menu with no Escape/outside-click/focus handling, no skip link, and low-contrast placeholder/helper text. SEO is functional but thin: only 2 of ~14 public/auth pages set their own title/description, there is no Open Graph/Twitter card, no metadataBase, and robots.ts allows crawling of private authed routes. No hard render-crash risks were found (all .toFixed calls are wrapped in Number(...), which yields a benign "NaN" string rather than throwing, and .map/.charAt sites are null-guarded), though a few money fields can display "$NaN".

findings.title: No error boundary anywhere (app/error.tsx & app/global-error.tsx missing)
findings.problem: There is no app/error.tsx or app/global-error.tsx. Every page is an async Server Component that awaits Supabase queries (e.g. bookings/[id]/page.tsx runs ~4 awaits, Nav.tsx queries notifications). If any query throws (network blip, RLS error, Supabase down) or a child component throws, React has no error boundary to catch it — in production the user sees the bare Next.js 'Application error: a client-side exception has occurred' page with no nav, footer, branding, or retry. There is no recovery path.
findings.fix: Add src/app/error.tsx ('use client') with a styled fallback and a reset() retry button, and src/app/global-error.tsx for root-layout-level failures (must render its own <html><body>). Consider per-section error.tsx for /bookings, /cleaner, /admin so a failure in one area doesn't blank the whole route.

findings.title: Primary/secondary buttons have no visible keyboard focus indicator (invalid CSS)
findings.problem: .btn-primary:focus and .btn-secondary:focus set 'outline: none' and then declare 'ring: 2px; ring-color: ...; ring-offset: 2px;'. ring/ring-color/ring-offset are Tailwind utility names, NOT real CSS properties, so the browser ignores them. Net effect: the outline is removed and nothing replaces it, leaving the main CTAs ('Book a Cleaning', 'Pay deposit', 'Accept', 'Submit Dispute', etc.) with zero visible focus state for keyboard users. Violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
findings.fix: Replace the invalid properties with real CSS, e.g. `.btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(5,150,105,0.45); }` and similar for btn-secondary. Prefer :focus-visible so the ring only shows for keyboard users. Add a global `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #059669; outline-offset: 2px; }` fallback.

findings.title: No custom 404 page (app/not-found.tsx missing) despite notFound() being used
findings.problem: bookings/[id]/page.tsx calls notFound() when a booking isn't owned by the user, and any unknown URL 404s. With no app/not-found.tsx, users hit the default unstyled Next.js 404 with no Nav/Footer, no branding, and no link back to safety. For a consumer marketplace this is a jarring dead end.
findings.fix: Add src/app/not-found.tsx rendering a branded 404 inside the shared layout with a heading, short copy, and links to '/' and '/book'.

findings.title: No route-level loading.tsx — navigations show no loading state
findings.problem: Every page is an async Server Component performing multiple sequential Supabase round-trips before first paint (e.g. bookings/[id] does booking + get_cleaner_card + address + reviews + messages). With no loading.tsx, navigation appears frozen on the previous page until all data resolves — no spinner or skeleton, hurting perceived performance, especially on mobile/slow networks.
findings.fix: Add loading.tsx at the app root and/or per heavy segment (/bookings, /bookings/[id], /cleaner, /admin) rendering a skeleton or spinner using the existing .card styles so Next streams an instant Suspense fallback.

findings.title: Most pages lack their own <title>/description (SEO)
findings.file: src/app/book/page.tsx, src/app/login/page.tsx (and all others except layout.tsx & about)
findings.problem: Only layout.tsx and about/page.tsx export metadata. Every other route — including the public, indexable /book and /login pages — inherits the generic root title 'Dust Busters — Home Cleaning in Courtenay, BC'. Search results and browser tabs can't distinguish pages, and the high-intent /book page has no targeted title/description.
findings.fix: Add `export const metadata = { title, description }` to server-component pages (book, bookings list, etc.). For client-component pages like login/page.tsx (which can't export metadata), introduce a login/layout.tsx that exports the metadata. Consider a title.template like '%s · Dust Busters' in the root layout.

findings.title: No Open Graph / Twitter card metadata and no metadataBase
findings.problem: Root metadata defines only title/description — no openGraph, no twitter, no metadataBase, and there is no opengraph-image (public/ only contains leftover Next scaffold SVGs and a default favicon.ico). Shared links (SMS, Messenger, Slack, X) render with no preview image, title, or card. Without metadataBase, any future OG/canonical URLs also won't resolve to absolute URLs.
findings.fix: In layout.tsx metadata add metadataBase: new URL(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3000'), plus openGraph {title, description, type:'website', locale:'en_CA', siteName} and twitter {card:'summary_large_image'}. Add an app/opengraph-image.png (or opengraph-image.tsx) and a real favicon/icon set.

findings.title: robots.ts lets crawlers index private/authenticated routes
findings.problem: robots returns a single rule allowing '/' with no disallow entries. Crawlers will follow links into /admin, /account, /bookings, /cleaner, /notifications, and /book — all auth-gated routes that just redirect to /login. This wastes crawl budget and risks indexing thin login-redirect/duplicate pages. sitemap.ts also lists /login, which shouldn't be in the sitemap.
findings.fix: Add disallow: ['/admin', '/account', '/bookings', '/cleaner', '/notifications', '/api', '/auth'] to the robots rule (keep allow:'/'). Remove '/login' from sitemap.ts and keep only genuinely public marketing pages ('/', '/about', '/book').

findings.title: Unlabeled textareas/inputs lack accessible names
findings.problem: The cancel-reason <textarea> (bookings/[id]/page.tsx) has only a placeholder and no <label>/aria-label. Both chat message controls (the textarea in components/MessagePanel.tsx and the input in bookings/[id]/MessagePanel.tsx) likewise rely on placeholder-only. Placeholders are not accessible names, so screen-reader users hear an unlabeled 'edit text' field, and the label disappears once typing begins (WCAG 1.3.1 / 4.1.2).
findings.fix: Add aria-label to each: cancel textarea -> aria-label="Reason for cancellation"; message fields -> aria-label="Type a message". (The dispute/review forms already use proper htmlFor labels — mirror that pattern.)

findings.title: Mobile nav menu: no Escape/outside-click close, missing aria-controls, no focus management
findings.problem: The new mobile hamburger menu toggles an absolutely-positioned dropdown. The toggle button has aria-expanded/aria-label (good) but no aria-controls/aria-haspopup, and the panel has no id. The menu does not close on Escape or on outside click, and focus is neither moved into the open panel nor returned to the button on close. Keyboard/screen-reader users can open it but have no standard way to dismiss it.
findings.fix: Give the panel an id and reference it via aria-controls on the button; add aria-haspopup="menu". Add a keydown handler closing on Escape (and returning focus to the toggle) and a document click/route-change listener to close on outside interaction. Existing per-link onClick close is fine to keep.

findings.title: No skip-to-content link for keyboard users
findings.problem: There is no 'Skip to main content' link, and the <main> elements have no id. Keyboard and screen-reader users must tab through the entire sticky nav (logo + all role-based links + notifications + logout) on every page before reaching content (WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks).
findings.fix:
Add a visually-hidden-until-focused anchor as the first body child in layout.tsx (e.g. `Skip to content`) and add id="main" to the content wrapper
(or to each page's
).

findings.title: Low color-contrast placeholder and helper text
findings.problem: .input-modern::placeholder is #cbd5e1 (slate-300) on white ≈ 1.5:1 contrast, far below the 4.5:1 minimum. Meaningful (non-incidental) text is also frequently rendered in text-slate-400 (#94a3b8 ≈ 2.5:1) — timestamps ('timeAgo'), 'Minimum 20 characters', 'net ~$x', empty-state messages — which fails WCAG 1.4.3.
findings.fix: Darken placeholder to ~slate-400/500 and switch meaningful helper/timestamp text from text-slate-400 to at least text-slate-500 (#64748b ≈ 4.8:1). Reserve slate-400 for truly decorative text.

findings.title: Money fields can render '$NaN' when amounts are null
findings.problem: These use `Number(booking.total_amount).toFixed(2)` without the `?? 0` guard used elsewhere. If the column is null/undefined (e.g. a booking still 'broadcasting' before amounts are computed, or a partial row), Number(undefined) is NaN and the UI shows '$NaN' / 'Pay $NaN deposit securely'. It does not crash, but it is a visible data/UX bug on the customer's payment CTA.
findings.fix: Apply the same guard used in admin/earnings pages: `Number(booking.total_amount ?? 0).toFixed(2)` (and likewise for deposit_amount/balance_amount), or hide the payment card until amounts exist.

findings.title: Decorative lucide icons not hidden from screen readers
findings.file: src/app/page.tsx (Shield/Clock/Star/...), and most pages using lucide-react
findings.problem: lucide-react renders <svg> without aria-hidden by default. The app uses dozens of purely decorative icons next to visible text labels; some screen readers announce these as 'image/graphic', adding noise. A few icon-only controls are correctly labeled, but the pervasive decorative icons are not marked decorative.
findings.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" (and focusable="false") to decorative icons, e.g. via a small wrapper or by passing aria-hidden on each icon. Keep accessible names only on icon-only interactive elements (already done for the nav bell and send button).

findings.title: Live status/notification updates are not announced to assistive tech
findings.problem: Realtime changes (cleaner found, deposit paid, new job offer) trigger a silent router.refresh(). The booking status badge text changes with no aria-live region, so screen-reader users get no notification that the state changed — a core part of the booking flow's value (real-time matching) is invisible to them.
findings.fix: Wrap the status badge in bookings/[id]/page.tsx with role="status" aria-live="polite" (and similarly announce new job offers), so the updated label is read out after the realtime refresh.

result.summary: The build toolchain is fundamentally sound: typecheck passes cleanly, the 3 unit tests pass, vitest is sensibly configured (sequential DB tests, generous timeouts), .gitignore correctly ignores all env files (no secret is committed), migrations are numerically ordered, and the 0007/0008 migrations show good idempotency intent (IF NOT EXISTS, unique-index upsert guard). However the project is NOT deploy-ready. Two runtime-blocking gaps exist: STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is empty (every webhook returns 400, so no booking ever advances past accepted/completed) and NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL is still localhost (Stripe success/cancel redirects, sitemap and robots all point at localhost in prod). A real data/schema defect: the Stripe charge.dispute.created webhook inserts columns (payment_id, stripe_dispute_id, reason, amount) that do not exist on the disputes table and omits its NOT NULL columns, so that handler always throws. next.config.ts is empty — no security headers (CSP/HSTS/etc.) and no image config. Surrounding deploy hygiene is missing: README is a one-liner, no .env.example, no vercel.json, no CI workflow, no coverage tooling, no engines pin, and migration 0008 has one non-idempotent ADD CONSTRAINT inconsistent with the rest of the file. The default `npm test` only runs 3 trivial pricing/role assertions; the payment/webhook and RLS logic have zero automated coverage in CI.

findings.title: STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET empty — all webhooks 400, bookings never advance after payment
findings.problem: STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is blank. In route.ts the handler calls stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(body, sig!, secret!) with secret undefined, which throws, returning HTTP 400 for every event. Since the booking status transitions (accepted->deposit_paid, completed->balance_paid) and the payments rows are written ONLY by this webhook, a production deploy with this unset means customers pay via Stripe Checkout but the booking is never marked paid, the address is never revealed to the cleaner, and no payment is recorded. This is a hard launch blocker.
findings.fix: Create the webhook endpoint in the Stripe dashboard (Developers -> Webhooks) for the deployed URL, subscribe to checkout.session.completed, charge.dispute.created, charge.refunded, copy the signing secret (whsec_...) into the Vercel env var STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, and add a startup/runtime guard that fails loudly if it is missing in production rather than returning a generic 400.

findings.title: NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL hardcoded to localhost — broken Stripe redirects, sitemap, robots in prod
findings.problem: NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000. This value feeds Stripe Checkout success_url/cancel_url (src/app/bookings/[id]/payment-actions.ts:50-51 via lib/stripe.baseUrl()), sitemap (src/app/sitemap.ts:3) and robots (src/app/robots.ts:3). If deployed without overriding it, after paying the customer is redirected to localhost (dead page), and the sitemap/robots advertise localhost URLs to crawlers. The lib/stripe.ts and sitemap/robots fallbacks all default to localhost too, so a forgotten env var fails silently instead of erroring.
findings.fix: Set NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL to the production origin (e.g. https://dustbusters.vercel.app or custom domain) in Vercel project env. Consider deriving it from VERCEL_PROJECT_PRODUCTION_URL as a safer default than localhost, and document it in .env.example.

findings.title: disputes table schema does not match the Stripe charge.dispute.created webhook insert (handler always throws)
findings.problem: The disputes table (0008) has columns: booking_id, raised_by (NOT NULL), category (NOT NULL, CHECK list), description (NOT NULL), status, resolution, resolved_at, created_at. The webhook inserts { booking_id, payment_id, stripe_dispute_id, reason, amount, status, created_at } — payment_id, stripe_dispute_id, reason and amount do not exist as columns, and it omits the NOT NULL raised_by/category/description. So every charge.dispute.created (Stripe chargeback) insert fails with a column/NOT-NULL error; it is swallowed by the try/catch and logged, meaning chargebacks silently never get recorded and the booking is left flagged 'disputed' with no dispute row. The status value passed from Stripe (e.g. 'needs_response') also violates the CHECK (open/investigating/resolved/closed).
findings.fix: Add a new migration that either (a) creates a dedicated stripe_disputes/chargebacks table (booking_id, payment_id, stripe_dispute_id unique, reason, amount, stripe_status, created_at), or (b) adds those columns to disputes and makes raised_by/category/description nullable for system-raised disputes. Then update the webhook to write the matching columns and map Stripe's status into the allowed enum. Add a regression test for the charge.dispute.created branch.

findings.title: Empty next.config.ts — no security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.)
findings.problem: nextConfig is an empty object. There is no headers() function and no middleware/proxy header logic (src/proxy.ts only refreshes Supabase sessions). The deployed app therefore ships with no Content-Security-Policy, no Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, no X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors (clickjacking), no Referrer-Policy and no Permissions-Policy. For a payment-handling marketplace this is a meaningful hardening gap and will fail most security/launch checklists.
findings.fix: Add an async headers() to next.config.ts returning a baseline set for all routes: Strict-Transport-Security (max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-Frame-Options: DENY (or CSP frame-ancestors 'none'), Permissions-Policy, and a CSP that allows self plus js.stripe.com / *.supabase.co (Stripe Checkout is a redirect so script-src can stay tight). Test that Stripe and Supabase realtime still work under the CSP.

findings.title: Migration 0008 ADD CONSTRAINT is not idempotent (inconsistent with the rest of the file)
findings.problem: Every other statement in 0008 is guarded for idempotency (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION), but ALTER TABLE bookings ADD CONSTRAINT bookings_cancelled_by_check ... NOT VALID has no guard. Postgres has no ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS, so re-applying this file (manual psql replay, partial-failure re-run, or applying against an already-migrated DB) errors with 'constraint already exists'. The constraint is also NOT VALID and never VALIDATEd, so existing rows are never checked.
findings.fix: Wrap it in a guard: DO $$ BEGIN IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname='bookings_cancelled_by_check') THEN ALTER TABLE bookings ADD CONSTRAINT bookings_cancelled_by_check CHECK (cancelled_by IN ('customer','cleaner','system','admin')) NOT VALID; END IF; END $$; and optionally add a follow-up ALTER TABLE ... VALIDATE CONSTRAINT once data is known clean.

findings.title: Default test script covers almost nothing; no CI workflow and no coverage tooling
findings.problem: npm test runs only vitest run tests/lib — 3 trivial assertions (computePrice math, ROLES array). The meaningful logic (RLS policies in tests/db/rls.test.ts, dispatch/accept-race/address-masking in tests/db/dispatch.test.ts) lives under test:db which is not part of test and requires a live remote Supabase + service-role key, so it cannot run in a standard CI runner. There is no .github/workflows, no coverage reporter (no @vitest/coverage-v8, no --coverage), and the entire Stripe webhook handler (including the broken disputes branch) has zero tests. Regressions in pricing, dispatch, payments and RLS would not be caught before deploy.
findings.fix: Add a GitHub Actions workflow running typecheck + vitest on push/PR. Add @vitest/coverage-v8 and a coverage:check script with thresholds. Refactor the webhook handler logic into a pure, unit-testable function (event -> intended DB mutations) so it can be tested without a live Stripe/Supabase, and spin up a Supabase service-container (or branch DB) so the db/rls suites can run in CI with secrets.

findings.title: README is a single non-informative line — no setup, env, migration, or deploy docs
findings.problem: README.md contains only 'Dust Buster! Lets GO!!!!'. A new developer or deployer has no documented list of required env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL/ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL), how to run migrations (supabase db push / reset), how to seed, how to create the first admin, how to run tests (test vs test:db), or the deploy steps and Stripe webhook setup. This compounds the launch risk because the two critical missing env values above are not documented anywhere.
findings.fix: Write a real README covering: stack overview, prerequisites, env var table, supabase migration + seed commands, admin bootstrap, dev/test commands, and a Vercel deploy section that explicitly lists setting NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and registering the Stripe webhook endpoint.

findings.title: No .env.example to document required environment variables for deploy
findings.problem: Only .env.local exists (correctly gitignored, containing real keys). There is no committed .env.example template, so the set of variables the app needs is undiscoverable without grepping the source. Combined with the localhost/empty-secret defaults that fail silently, deployers are very likely to ship with missing or wrong values.
findings.fix: Commit a .env.example with every required key, blank values, and a one-line comment per key (which are public NEXT_PUBLIC_* vs server-only secrets). Reference it from the README. Keep .env.example out of the .gitignore env-file patterns.

findings.title: No engines field — Node version not pinned for Next 16 / React 19
findings.problem: package.json has no engines field and no packageManager field. Next 16 and React 19 require a modern Node (>=20.9). On Vercel or another host without a pinned version, a default/older Node could be selected, producing build or runtime differences from the local environment (local is Node 24).
findings.fix: Add "engines": { "node": ">=20.9.0" } (and optionally a "packageManager" field) to package.json and/or a .nvmrc, so CI, Vercel, and contributors all build on a compatible Node.

findings.title: metadataBase not set — OG/canonical URLs resolve to localhost and build warns
findings.problem: The root metadata export defines title/description but no metadataBase. Next.js resolves relative metadata URLs (Open Graph images, canonical, etc.) against http://localhost:3000 and emits a build-time warning. Any future OG image or canonical link will point at localhost in production.
findings.fix: Set metadataBase: new URL(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3000') in the layout metadata, and add basic openGraph/twitter metadata while you are there.

findings.title: No image optimization config in next.config (remotePatterns) for future Supabase storage images
findings.problem: next.config.ts defines no images config. Today only local SVGs in /public are used (no next/image with remote sources), so nothing is broken yet, but the moment cleaner avatars or uploaded photos are served from Supabase Storage, next/image will reject the remote host without an images.remotePatterns allowlist, and there is no formats/deviceSizes tuning.
findings.fix: Pre-add images.remotePatterns for the Supabase storage hostname ({ protocol:'https', hostname:'<project>.supabase.co', pathname:'/storage/v1/object/public/**' }) so avatar/photo features work when added, and consider setting formats: ['image/avif','image/webp'].

findings.title: No vercel.json — build/region/function settings and headers undocumented
findings.problem: There is no vercel.json. Next on Vercel works without one, but there is no committed declaration of region (Supabase project is single-region; co-locating functions reduces latency for the realtime/webhook paths), function maxDuration, or platform-level headers/redirects, and no infra-as-code record of the deploy target.
findings.fix: Add a minimal vercel.json pinning the deploy region close to the Supabase project region and, if needed, function maxDuration for the Stripe webhook route. Keep security headers in next.config.ts (preferred) rather than duplicating here.

findings.title: Marketing pages are forced dynamic — no static generation / revalidation strategy
findings.problem: Public, content-static routes (/, /about) render through the same auth-aware server tree and are effectively dynamic on every request; there is no export const revalidate or static rendering anywhere in the app. For pages whose content does not depend on the user, this wastes server compute and hurts TTFB/cache hit rate on Vercel's edge.
findings.fix: For genuinely static marketing routes, avoid per-request auth reads in the page itself (move user-aware UI into the Nav/client island) and add export const revalidate = <seconds> or render them statically so they can be CDN-cached, while keeping authenticated dashboards dynamic.

findings.title: Refund handling incomplete and payment_status enum has no 'refunded' state
findings.problem: The charge.refunded webhook branch only console.logs; it does not update the payments row or the booking. The payment_status enum is ('pending','paid','failed') with no 'refunded' value, so even if the handler tried to record a refund there is no terminal state to set, and refunds/partial refunds leave the data showing the payment as still 'paid'.
findings.fix: Add a migration extending payment_status with 'refunded' (and optionally a refunded_amount column), then update the charge.refunded handler to mark the matching payment refunded and reflect it on the booking/admin views.
